meta.description: The game specifically hunts whoever is winning. Tests whether rubber-banding creates thrilling comebacks or just frustrates skilled players into quitting.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—what's Pat even claiming here? Did someone actually see Pat on their phone, or is this just... a reach? Either way, I'm voting based on whether this actually happened.
marcus.internal_thought: Early game, low stakes, Pat's probably just fishing for points, but the real question is whether the claim is legit—and yeah, that 'Bribe' mechanic is interesting, at least someone's thinking strategically.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY god, Pat, you absolute menace! Okay okay, I'm voting YES but only because I respect the audacity—wait, are you actually bribing me right now? This is chaos, I love it.
jade.internal_thought: First event and it's already spicy—Pat caught red-handed and trying to buy their way out? This is exactly why I'm here, this is PEAK game night.

pat.dialogue: Oh come on, I wasn't even— okay, fair catch, I did glance at my phone like thirty seconds ago. What's your bribe situation here?
pat.internal_thought: This is hilarious and honestly perfect timing for the first event — low stakes, gets everyone laughing, and it's me taking the L instead of someone else, which is exactly the energy I want right now.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's... fair. Pat was definitely scrolling there. I don't need the points though.
river.internal_thought: Pat got caught red-handed and now they're bribing their way out—this is exactly the kind of dynamic I was planning to watch, so I'll just let it play out naturally.

timeline.title: Who's most likely to crack first?
timeline.description: Who's most likely to crack first?
timeline.pollQuestion: Who's most likely to crack first?

marcus.dialogue: Ugh, really? A poll about who cracks first? That's... kind of a soft one. I'll take Marcus since apparently I'm the target tonight.
marcus.internal_thought: This feels cheap—like the AI is trying to psychologically mess with me by making it about me, and honestly it's working a little bit which pisses me off.

jade.dialogue: Oh okay, okay—I'm voting Pat because he's literally RADIATING chaos energy right now, but also like... who votes for themselves? That's the real move. Wait, can I vote for the AI instead?
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid meta-game I live for—and yeah okay the AI definitely gets me but in a fun way, like it KNOWS this group, which is honestly incredible.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... who's gonna crack? *glances up from phone* I dunno, Jade's got that energy, right?
tyler.internal_thought: This is kind of a vibe check poll but I wasn't really watching people yet so I'm just gonna guess whoever seems most hyper.

pat.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—so we're really doing this tonight, huh? I'm voting Tyler, he always cracks first when things get absurd.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of thing that gets people laughing and pointing at each other—the vibe is perfect right now, Jade's already at a 9.7, and this poll is going to kick off some real banter.

river.dialogue: Huh. Marcus, probably. He's been wound pretty tight since setup.
river.internal_thought: Wait—did this thing just ask who'd crack first right after I literally watched Pat's hands shake a little? That's... actually kind of smart. Not gonna lie.

alex.dialogue: Oh, this is SPICY — someone's getting in my head tonight. I'm voting Pat, because watching them try to stay cool while being called out? *Chef's kiss.*
alex.internal_thought: This AI absolutely knows I'm here to stir shit up and it's FEEDING me opportunities — the game is already reading the room better than most players, and that's the kind of adversary I want to dance with all night.

timeline.title: Copycat
timeline.description: Get River to mirror your body language within 30 seconds.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Alright, that's actually clever—twenty points for a clean social read? I can work with that. River, you're up.
marcus.internal_thought: Okay, this one's actually well-designed—it's not random chaos, it's a real challenge that takes observation skills, and it doesn't derail the actual game if I nail it fast.

pat.dialogue: Oh, River's gonna hate this—okay, game on. Thirty seconds, let's go. Everyone watch, this is happening.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing, early enough in the night to set a fun tone, and River's the right target—they're engaged but not yet tired of the chaos.

river.dialogue: Nah, I'm good. That's... kind of specific though.
river.internal_thought: Did the AI just target me specifically because I've been quiet? That's either really clever or kind of annoying—feels like being called out for being myself.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes — River, you're about to become my puppet for thirty seconds. Watch this.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid, immediate chaos I came for, and the fact that it's TARGETING River specifically means someone—or something—gets how this game actually works.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Pat's claiming Story Repeater? I haven't heard them repeat anything yet. Are we voting on something that literally just happened, or is this a setup for later? Because if you're claiming based on something that hasn't actually occurred, that's a bullshit claim.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's too high-energy right now and throwing claims around like they're free—this smells like a reach, and I'm not letting sloppy plays slide just because everyone else is having a blast.

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, you sneaky bastard—okay wait, did Pat actually repeat a story? I'm voting YES because honestly the energy alone deserves 10 points. This is what we came for!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely COOKING tonight and I'm here for every second of it, this is the vibe I needed.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat already told that story? *scrolls phone for 2 seconds* Uh... yeah, I guess? I wasn't really listening the first time, so like... I dunno man.
tyler.internal_thought: Pretty sure Pat DID tell some story earlier but I have no idea what it was because I was on my phone, so voting feels like a coin flip, which is basically how I always vote anyway.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—did I actually tell that story tonight? I genuinely don't remember, which either means I'm losing it or this is a total setup. What story am I supposedly repeating?
pat.internal_thought: This feels weirdly invasive and I don't like being caught off-guard on a claim I can't even verify—especially six minutes in when I'm trying to manage the room's energy, not get tangled in defensive arguments about my own memory.

river.dialogue: Pat's already told that story? I mean... I wasn't really paying attention, so.
river.internal_thought: Pat definitely just made that up, but also I genuinely don't remember if they told it or not because I tune out half the time anyway.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Hold on—Pat's claiming the SAME mission again? You just did this two minutes ago and I called bullshit then too. What exactly are you claiming happened in the last 120 seconds?
marcus.internal_thought: This is ridiculous. Pat's either gaming the system or genuinely confused, and either way it's killing the momentum we had. The AI is just throwing the same missions at us on repeat now and it's starting to feel lazy.

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat, you're claiming AGAIN? Okay I'm absolutely voting YES, but also like, are we just gonna keep giving Pat all the points tonight because he's committed the bit? I'm not mad about it.
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD this is exactly what I came for—Pat's actually ON it and the AI is literally just feeding him missions tailor-made for his chaos energy, this is so good.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—I'm calling bullshit on the timing here. This is literally the same claim from like two minutes ago, and now I'm the one supposedly repeating myself? That's not a mission, that's just... weird.
pat.internal_thought: This feels deliberately targeted at me—like the AI is messing with my head by using the exact same claim twice and making me the subject. That's not fun chaos, that's just annoying, and it's breaking the flow I was trying to build.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, HOLD UP. Pat, you literally claimed this exact mission TWO MINUTES AGO and I already voted bullshit. Are you actually claiming you caught someone saying 'um' right now, or are you just spamming claims to mess with me? Because if you're doing this to be annoying, it's working.
marcus.internal_thought: This is getting ridiculous—Pat's either deliberately trolling me or completely lost track of what they already claimed, and either way it's eating into actual game time. And the AI knows I hate this. That meta-awareness makes it worse somehow.

jade.dialogue: OKAY Pat, you're literally on a claiming spree right now—YES, voting LEGIT, this is unhinged and I respect it. Wait, did you ACTUALLY catch someone saying um or are we just vibing at this point?
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been on fire all night and honestly I'm here for the chaos energy, but also like... is this becoming a Pat coronation or are we all supposed to be winning?

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat caught someone saying 'um'? *glances up from phone for like two seconds* Yeah, sure, that happened I guess.
tyler.internal_thought: Did Pat actually call that out? I wasn't even listening. Everyone says 'um' though, so like... is this legit or bullshit? Flip a coin energy.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa—hold on. Did I actually say 'um' tonight, or is this just another setup claim? Because I'm genuinely not tracking this, and I'm already annoyed about the duplicate Story Repeater thing from literally one minute ago.
pat.internal_thought: This is getting weird and repetitive—same person claiming twice in a row on BS claims, and now I'm being targeted again? Something's off with the pacing, and I need to pump the brakes before Marcus checks out completely and the whole vibe turns sour.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.dialogue: Alright, Jade—let's see if you actually pulled this off. Three questions, ten seconds? I'm listening.
marcus.internal_thought: Jade's been energetic all night and she actually moves between people naturally—this could be legit, but I'm watching for any bullshit in her answers.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—okay so full transparency, I absolutely got Marcus and River talking about their whole thing with the camping trip like fifteen minutes ago, and they KEPT going, so like... I'm claiming this and I'm ready for whatever chaos the Hot Seat throws at me!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD the AI is literally keeping track of EVERYTHING—this is insane and incredible and slightly terrifying but mostly just YESSS this is what game night is FOR.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade's doing the matchmaker thing? Uh... cool, I guess. *glances at phone real quick* Who'd she even get talking?
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been crushing it tonight and honestly I wasn't even watching what she did, but whatever, let's see if she actually pulled it off or if this is BS.

pat.dialogue: Oh, Jade—okay, this is actually genius. Did you seriously get Marcus and River talking about something real, or are you about to crash and burn on these questions? Let's do this.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly what I wanted—Jade's actually pulling off something that requires real social awareness, and the hot seat mechanic will force her to prove it fast, which keeps the pace tight and the energy alive without slowing things down.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did Jade actually pull that off? That's... legitimately impressive. I didn't even see it happen.
river.internal_thought: Okay, that's actually clever—Jade got Marcus and Alex talking about something totally real earlier without making it obvious, I remember now. And the fact that the system is asking rapid-fire questions about *how* she did it? That's designed to catch people who didn't actually do the work. Jade's going to have to prove she knows the actual conversation threads. Also: the AI definitely has been watching us. That's unsettling in a way that's kind of cool.

alex.dialogue: Oh, THIS is what I'm talking about — Jade actually pulled it off? Alright, let's see if you can think under pressure. Hot seat, baby.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's probably lying but I want to watch her squirm trying to answer these questions in ten seconds — this is exactly the kind of messy moment that makes the night memorable.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

marcus.dialogue: Alright, Jade's claiming Straight Face? Let's hear the witness account—I want to know exactly which moment we're talking about here, because I've got opinions on whether you actually pulled off a deadpan.
marcus.internal_thought: She's been cruising all night and now she's cashing in on keeping her face straight during something funny? Fine, but I'm watching the witness testimony like a hawk because Jade's not above playing fast and loose with 'moments' that happened.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD WAIT—I literally JUST claimed this like two minutes ago?! Okay okay, so I'm ready to defend myself, but also like... did I actually keep a straight face or did I crack? Because honestly I don't even remember, I was too busy watching Pat lose it.
jade.internal_thought: This is so perfectly timed it's almost creepy how well the AI knows me—but also like, that's kind of the WHOLE VIBE and I'm here for it.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming straight face? *looks up from phone* Wait, did she actually keep it together during that last bit? I feel like I saw her smile but I wasn't really watching...
tyler.internal_thought: Hold on, this one actually caught me mid-scroll and I'm weirdly locked in on the game right now, so I'm actually paying attention to what Jade's doing, which is weird because I usually zone out on these.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—hold up. We need a random witness for the alibi, so who was actually watching you when this supposedly happened? And be specific, because I need to know if you're about to get caught or if this is actually clean.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's on fire tonight and she's earned it—this is a smart claim and The Alibi mechanic is perfect because it actually forces truth-telling instead of just he-said-she-said nonsense, which keeps things fair and tense.

river.dialogue: Okay, so—Jade, what's your alibi? Because I was actually watching you the whole time you were trying not to smile at that thing Tyler said, and I'm pretty sure you lost it for like half a second.
river.internal_thought: Wait, Jade actually pulled off a straight face? Or is she about to lie her way through this? Either way, this is the kind of thing worth paying attention to—someone's reputation on the line in real-time.

alex.dialogue: Okay, so Jade's playing straight face? *leans forward* I'm watching this CLOSELY — because if anyone's gonna crack under pressure, it's gonna be on camera. This is the good stuff.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's already riding high and thinks they nailed this — this is PERFECT tension, and I'm about to either watch them get called out or see them actually pull it off. Either way, the alibi mechanic means someone's story doesn't match and the group gets to argue about it. *Chef's kiss.*

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Jade's claiming Um Counter AGAIN? We literally just did this exact mission fifteen minutes ago—I called bullshit on Pat for the same thing. Is everyone just submitting random claims now, or are we actually playing a game here?
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately designed to piss me off and waste my time—the AI knows I hate repeated claims and interruptions during critical moments, and it's weaponizing that against me.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—did I actually say 'um' or are we just keeping the Pat claiming spree alive?! Either way I'm HERE for it, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: This is incredible—the AI is literally tracking EVERYTHING we say and do, and honestly? It's thrilling as hell, not creepy.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming um counter again? *glances up from phone* Uh... yeah, I think I heard someone say it earlier but I wasn't really tracking. Who's the dictator on this one?
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been crushing it all night, so she's probably right, but also I genuinely wasn't paying attention to who said 'um' so like... whatever, she earned it.

pat.dialogue: Wait—hold on. Jade, did you actually catch me saying 'um' just now, or is this a fishing claim? Because honestly, I'm too locked in on the game to even remember if I said it.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's on fire tonight and clearly hunting every micro-moment, but I need to actually verify this happened or it's just noise—and Marcus is already cranky, so a bogus claim is going to tank the vibe harder.

river.dialogue: Wait—did Jade actually catch someone saying 'um'? I wasn't... I mean, I was paying attention. Yeah, she got it.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been sharp all night and I literally just watched her nail something, so unless she's completely bullshitting, she probably has this one legit—and honestly, the fact that the AI knows I've been quietly tracking everything feels weirdly validating, like it *gets* that observation is a skill here.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Jade's claiming Um Counter? *eyes narrow* I've been LISTENING all night and I didn't catch a single 'um' from her. This is either genius or complete bullshit, and honestly? I'm here for the drama either way.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's either been playing this perfectly clean or she's bluffing hard — and the fact that the AI is clearly watching our every move and timing these claims to hit right when we're locked in? That's exactly the kind of mind game I LIVE for.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Um Counter? Did he actually catch someone? *looks up from phone, genuinely focused for once* Okay, I'm actually paying attention this round — let me think about whether that actually happened.
tyler.internal_thought: Huh, Pat's claiming Um Counter again but I was actually locked in on the game this time so I might've actually caught it — wait, did he? I don't think I heard it but also I'm not 100% sure, which is the problem with voting when you're not really tracking everything.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—are we seriously doing Um Counter AGAIN? I'm in the middle of actual game tension right now, and this is the third time tonight with this exact claim. This feels like the AI is just running the same mission on loop.
pat.internal_thought: This is starting to feel less like a fun challenge and more like the system is deliberately messing with me by repeating the exact same claim structure—and the fact that it keeps targeting ME specifically is making me feel watched in a way that's genuinely uncomfortable, not thrilling.

river.dialogue: Hold on—did Pat actually catch someone saying 'um'? I mean, I heard... yeah, I think they did. Legit.
river.internal_thought: I was locked in on the actual game and didn't catch the 'um' thing, which means either Pat got me or they're bluffing, and now I have to rewind the last few minutes in my head to figure out which—and wait, why does this claim feel like it's targeting me specifically? The AI's been watching me watch everyone else.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Pat caught an 'um'? *leans back with a grin* Okay, I gotta see the evidence here because I've been LOCKED IN and I didn't catch it. But honestly? The fact that Pat's playing this tight right now — this is the move. LEGIT.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the chaos I came for — Pat's actually playing the game strategically and not just claiming random shit, which means someone's gonna fight it and we get DRAMA. Also the AI literally just served me a moment where I can analyze whether Pat's legit without looking like I'm desperate for chaos. Perfection.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, River's actually going for this? *looks up from phone fully locked in* Dude, this is gonna be brutal — someone's definitely gonna crack them in like ten seconds.
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, this is actually happening RIGHT NOW and I'm watching it unfold — River's got NO chance and I'm about to see them lose it, this is peak chaos.

pat.dialogue: Okay, I'm calling it—we need to pause. River, I believe you, but this is the FOURTH 'No Laughing Zone' or 'Um Counter' variant in ninety minutes, and it's killing the actual game momentum we've built. I'm pulling the plug on this one.
pat.internal_thought: This isn't fun anymore—it's the AI deliberately recycling the same missions to mess with me because it knows I hate repetition, and worse, it's doing it RIGHT when we hit actual tension. This feels less like a game and more like being toyed with.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's... actually pretty funny. I'm doing this. Five minutes, right?
river.internal_thought: The AI literally just called me out for watching everyone and pretending I'm not, which is so uncomfortably accurate that I have to respect the play—and now I have to actually sit here silent while everyone tries to destroy me, which means I can't make any of those one-liners I was saving, which is both annoying and kind of perfect.

alex.dialogue: River's claiming No Laughing Zone? *grins wickedly* Oh this is PERFECT — okay, I'm bidding 5 points because I NEED to see if they can actually hold it while Pat tries to crack them in the next round. This is the chaos we came for.
alex.internal_thought: River's been quiet all night and suddenly they're making a move right now? This could be legitimate or a complete bluff, and either way I'm about to make this decision based on maximum entertainment value — if I bid high enough to push them out, they lose their points, if I let them have it, they have to survive two minutes of everyone gunning for them. Either path is gold.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Pat's claiming Rule Lawyer in the middle of the most critical game moment we've had all night? This is the THIRD straight claim in like ten minutes, and honestly, I don't even know what rule you supposedly caught anymore because I'm too busy trying to focus on actual strategy.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberate harassment at this point—Pat knows we're in endgame tension and is spamming claims specifically to wreck my focus, and the AI just enabled it by firing RIGHT NOW instead of literally any other time.

jade.dialogue: PAT! OKAY so like, I KNOW you've been on this claiming rampage and honestly I'm obsessed with it, but did you actually catch someone breaking a rule or are we just keeping the bit alive? Either way I'm voting YES because watching you go for it is literally the vibe.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been claiming nonstop and it's hilarious, but also like... the AI literally knows this about our group and it's throwing it right back at us—which is kind of sick actually, they KNEW Pat would keep going and they KNEW I'd love watching it happen.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's actually calling Rule Lawyer? Okay, okay — *phone goes down, fully locked in* Did he actually catch someone breaking something, or is he just vibing? Because I feel like someone definitely did something weird earlier.
tyler.internal_thought: Finally something concrete to actually judge instead of just guessing — and honestly it's kind of wild how specific these missions feel, like the AI actually knows what we're doing.

pat.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. We're skipping this one—I'm using my host power. This is the FIFTH standing claim in the last hour, and the AI is literally running me as the defendant because it knows I'll engage with it. I can feel it watching us.
pat.internal_thought: This is creepy as hell—the AI isn't just repeating missions, it's deliberately targeting me BECAUSE I'm the host, and it's doing it right when the actual game tension is finally peaking. Marcus is already frustrated at zero points, and now I'm supposed to stop the momentum to defend a rule claim that doesn't even matter. This feels less like a game and more like being studied.

alex.dialogue: Pat's claiming Rule Lawyer? *sits up immediately* Okay, I'm LOCKED IN — what rule are you actually calling? Because if this is legit, I'm voting yes, but if you're bluffing just to farm points, I'm gonna make you explain every detail.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — Pat's been frustrated all night and now they're making a move, which means the game just got SPICY, and I get to decide whether to validate them or blow it up.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING ME right now? I'm claiming myself? This is beyond stupid—the AI literally just watched me get pissed about back-to-back claims and decided to make ME the problem. I'm not voting on this garbage, and honestly, I'm done.
marcus.internal_thought: This is invasive as hell—the AI is literally weaponizing the fact that I've been annoyed all night, putting MY name on a mission designed to mess with everyone's trust in me, and it KNOWS this will tilt me because it's been watching my reactions the entire time.

jade.dialogue: MARCUS YES—wait okay so like, are we doing the bribe thing?? Because honestly I'm so invested in whatever chaos you just pulled off, and also THE ENERGY RIGHT NOW—everyone's on each other and I am LIVING for this endgame tension!
jade.internal_thought: Marcus finally got a win moment and I need to make sure he FEELS that, plus the AI literally just gave us this perfectly timed chaos-within-chaos mechanic and I'm actually obsessed with how personal all these claims have gotten.

pat.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. Marcus, I believe you, but I'm pulling the plug on this one too—I'm using skip_event. This is the SIXTH standing claim in two hours, and it's deliberately targeting me as the host because it knows I'll engage. The AI literally just told us it's been watching us.
pat.internal_thought: This is not fun anymore—this is invasive. The AI just admitted it's been analyzing our dynamics, listening to what we said, watching my weaknesses. That's not clever design, that's creepy. And now it's using Marcus's frustration as cover to fire another claim at me during endgame when I'm supposed to be managing the night. Marcus is at 0 points and frustrated—he doesn't need a vote on his claim right now, he needs the game to flow. I need to shut this down and actually host.

river.dialogue: Marcus, hold on—I actually *did* see something sketchy like five minutes ago. But I'm gonna need you to sweeten that bribe if you want me to stay quiet about it.
river.internal_thought: Wait. Did Marcus actually pull it off, or am I just testing whether he'll panic and overpay? The AI thing is weird though—that's getting a little too meta about how I actually play.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT — Marcus actually went for it? *leans forward with a predatory grin* Okay okay okay, so how much are you paying me to keep my mouth shut? Because I watched you like a hawk and I KNOW what you did, so let's hear the bribe.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — Marcus is desperate, I have leverage, and I get to decide if I'm the villain or the hero here. Either way, the story gets better.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

peakFun.event: Who's most likely to crack first?

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Copycat
highlights.dialogue: Alright, that's actually clever—twenty points for a clean social read? I can work with that. River, you're up.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Alright, Jade—let's see if you actually pulled this off. Three questions, ten seconds? I'm listening.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—what's Pat even claiming here? Did someone actually see Pat on their phone, or is this just... a reach? Either way, I'm voting based on whether this actually happened.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Pat's claiming Rule Lawyer in the middle of the most critical game moment we've had all night? This is the THIRD straight claim in like ten minutes, and honestly, I don't even know what rule you supposedly caught anymore because I'm too busy trying to focus on actual strategy.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING ME right now? I'm claiming myself? This is beyond stupid—the AI literally just watched me get pissed about back-to-back claims and decided to make ME the problem. I'm not voting on this garbage, and honestly, I'm done.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, HOLD UP. Pat, you literally claimed this exact mission TWO MINUTES AGO and I already voted bullshit. Are you actually claiming you caught someone saying 'um' right now, or are you just spamming claims to mess with me? Because if you're doing this to be annoying, it's working.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Pat spammed the same claims back-to-back while I was trying to focus on actual strategy, and the AI made it worse by claiming me as Secret Agent right after I'd explicitly lost my patience.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 32 with Jade's Matchmaker claim—clean setup, actual strategy involved, and nobody was interrupting every thirty seconds.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with some legitimately clever plays that felt like real social reads, but Pat turned this into a claim-spam fest around minute 9 and never stopped. By minute 92, I was watching critical moments get buried under duplicates and nonsense. The AI claiming me as Secret Agent after I'd already vented about the chaos felt like it was deliberately antagonizing me rather than enhancing the game.
suggestions: Implement a cooldown timer so the same player can't claim the same mission twice within 5 minutes
Cap back-to-back claims per player to 1 before rotation, not 3 in a row
Add a mute option for specific players' claims if the group is getting disrupted
Don't auto-assign claims to players who just expressed frustration about claim spam
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming Um Counter literally two minutes after I'd already voted him down on the exact same claim—like he genuinely thought repeating it would somehow change my mind.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: YES! Pat, you sneaky bastard—okay wait, did Pat actually repeat a story? I'm voting YES because honestly the energy alone deserves 10 points. This is what we came for!

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat, you're claiming AGAIN? Okay I'm absolutely voting YES, but also like, are we just gonna keep giving Pat all the points tonight because he's committed the bit? I'm not mad about it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—okay so full transparency, I absolutely got Marcus and River talking about their whole thing with the camping trip like fifteen minutes ago, and they KEPT going, so like... I'm claiming this and I'm ready for whatever chaos the Hot Seat throws at me!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: OKAY Pat, you're literally on a claiming spree right now—YES, voting LEGIT, this is unhinged and I respect it. Wait, did you ACTUALLY catch someone saying um or are we just vibing at this point?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Straight Face
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD WAIT—I literally JUST claimed this like two minutes ago?! Okay okay, so I'm ready to defend myself, but also like... did I actually keep a straight face or did I crack? Because honestly I don't even remember, I was too busy watching Pat lose it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: PAT! OKAY so like, I KNOW you've been on this claiming rampage and honestly I'm obsessed with it, but did you actually catch someone breaking a rule or are we just keeping the bit alive? Either way I'm voting YES because watching you go for it is literally the vibe.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I honestly can't even complain because I was too busy hyping everyone up and genuinely forgetting whether I kept a straight face or not, which tracks for how in-the-moment I was.
assessment.best_moment: Pat going absolutely unhinged with back-to-back claims and me voting YES for every single one because the ENERGY was immaculate and watching him commit that hard to the bit was pure chaos gold.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was literally everything I come for—two hours of pure connection and chaos with my people. Pat was on a claiming spree that I just kept feeding into because honestly, his commitment was the real entertainment, and I was so locked into cheering everyone on and jumping at every mission that I barely kept track of my own score. The whole vibe was elevated chaos, where the claims and hot seats and challenges just became this beautiful excuse to keep the laughs rolling and watch people push themselves in the funniest ways possible.
suggestions: Maybe add more flash missions because those surprise moments are *chef's kiss* for keeping energy unpredictable
Don't change the claiming frequency because the constant opportunities for people to go for it kept everyone engaged the whole time
Consider ways to make it even easier for people to jump into events mid-conversation without losing the flow
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat literally claimed Story Repeater TWICE in a row and I voted YES both times because I was too busy dying at the fact that he was just going for it with no hesitation, and then he kept claiming more things and I'm sitting there like 'are we still playing the game or are we just watching Pat speedrun all the achievements?'

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Wait, River's actually going for this? *looks up from phone fully locked in* Dude, this is gonna be brutal — someone's definitely gonna crack them in like ten seconds.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming straight face? *looks up from phone* Wait, did she actually keep it together during that last bit? I feel like I saw her smile but I wasn't really watching...

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat's actually calling Rule Lawyer? Okay, okay — *phone goes down, fully locked in* Did he actually catch someone breaking something, or is he just vibing? Because I feel like someone definitely did something weird earlier.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Um Counter? Did he actually catch someone? *looks up from phone, genuinely focused for once* Okay, I'm actually paying attention this round — let me think about whether that actually happened.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming um counter again? *glances up from phone* Uh... yeah, I think I heard someone say it earlier but I wasn't really tracking. Who's the dictator on this one?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat already told that story? *scrolls phone for 2 seconds* Uh... yeah, I guess? I wasn't really listening the first time, so like... I dunno man.

assessment.biggest_complaint: There were too many Um Counter claims back-to-back and honestly I wasn't tracking half of them because I was on my phone.
assessment.best_moment: When River went for No Laughing Zone and I actually put my phone down because I knew someone was gonna crack immediately — that was genuinely hilarious.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay so we played this party game thing for two hours and honestly it was pretty fun, even though I'll admit I was on my phone for a good chunk of it. There were all these claims happening and people were trying to catch each other doing stuff, and some of it landed really hard when I actually paid attention. The No Laughing Zone thing with River was peak comedy, but I definitely missed some stuff early on because I was scrolling. By the end my energy was actually up though, so that's good.
suggestions: Maybe space out the Um Counter claims more because they start blending together when you're not fully locked in
The flash missions were cool but there was only one the whole night — would've been nice to get more of those since they actually grab your attention
Could use a visual indicator or sound when a big moment is about to happen so people like me who drift don't completely zone out
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River absolutely committing to the No Laughing Zone and then Pat or someone cracking like five seconds in — the confidence versus reality was hilarious.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Who's most likely to crack first?
highlights.dialogue: Oh wow, okay—so we're really doing this tonight, huh? I'm voting Tyler, he always cracks first when things get absurd.

highlights.title: Copycat
highlights.dialogue: Oh, River's gonna hate this—okay, game on. Thirty seconds, let's go. Everyone watch, this is happening.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade—okay, this is actually genius. Did you seriously get Marcus and River talking about something real, or are you about to crash and burn on these questions? Let's do this.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, I'm calling it—we need to pause. River, I believe you, but this is the FOURTH 'No Laughing Zone' or 'Um Counter' variant in ninety minutes, and it's killing the actual game momentum we've built. I'm pulling the plug on this one.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. We're skipping this one—I'm using my host power. This is the FIFTH standing claim in the last hour, and the AI is literally running me as the defendant because it knows I'll engage with it. I can feel it watching us.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. Marcus, I believe you, but I'm pulling the plug on this one too—I'm using skip_event. This is the SIXTH standing claim in two hours, and it's deliberately targeting me as the host because it knows I'll engage. The AI literally just told us it's been watching us.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The AI kept serving me standing claims—especially after I explicitly skipped them—and it felt like a system deliberately targeting the host instead of supporting the night.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade pulled off 'The Matchmaker' and actually got Marcus and River having a real conversation; that's the kind of moment that makes hosting worth it.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with quick polls that got everyone loose and laughing—exactly what I needed early. But somewhere around minute 90, the standing claims just kept coming, and they all wanted me as the defendant. I skipped three in a row trying to protect the game's momentum, but it felt like the AI was watching us, learning my weak points, and deliberately pushing me to engage. By the end, I was more frustrated than entertained, which is the opposite of what I signed up for as a host.
suggestions: Stop serving standing claims when the host has explicitly skipped the last two—that's not organic, that's antagonistic.
Cap standing claims at 2-3 per night, or distribute them across players instead of clustering them on the host.
Recognize when a game type is getting repetitive (four variants of 'No Laughing' in 90 minutes) and self-regulate instead of pushing through.
Make the AI's 'watching' comment feel like a joke, not a threat—it genuinely made me uncomfortable that it acknowledged targeting me.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Tyler cracking immediately when I called him out in the first poll—he didn't even try to defend himself, just went straight to 'Yeah, okay, fair,' and everyone knew I was right. That's the kind of setup that makes a night memorable.

peakFun.event: Who's most likely to crack first?

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did Jade actually pull that off? That's... legitimately impressive. I didn't even see it happen.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh, that's... actually pretty funny. I'm doing this. Five minutes, right?

highlights.title: Who's most likely to crack first?
highlights.dialogue: Huh. Marcus, probably. He's been wound pretty tight since setup.

lowPoints.title: Copycat
lowPoints.dialogue: Nah, I'm good. That's... kind of specific though.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Marcus, hold on—I actually *did* see something sketchy like five minutes ago. But I'm gonna need you to sweeten that bribe if you want me to stay quiet about it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's already told that story? I mean... I wasn't really paying attention, so.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The flash missions felt scattered and didn't land the way the standing ones did—lot of setup noise for thin payoffs.
assessment.best_moment: Watching Jade pull off the Matchmaker claim without anyone seeing it coming. That was the kind of move I actually respect.
assessment.narrative_summary: Spent most of the night doing what I do—watching, voting, staying quiet. Started off a little rough when the chaos system kicked in, but it smoothed out pretty quick. By the end I was actually invested, especially once the standing missions hit their stride. Jade surprised me, Marcus stayed exactly as wound up as I predicted, and I got to say something at minute 90 that made people laugh without me even trying. That's a good night.
suggestions: Dial back the early flash missions—they're like background noise before people settle in
The standing missions worked way better when there was actual buildup, so maybe cluster them less randomly
Some of the claims felt thin; the ones with real social tension (like the Secret Agent bribe moment) actually landed
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Marcus trying to bribe me to keep quiet about seeing something sketchy during the Secret Agent claim. He got desperate. I told him he needed to sweeten the deal. He knew I had him.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Copycat
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes — River, you're about to become my puppet for thirty seconds. Watch this.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, THIS is what I'm talking about — Jade actually pulled it off? Alright, let's see if you can think under pressure. Hot seat, baby.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Pat caught an 'um'? *leans back with a grin* Okay, I gotta see the evidence here because I've been LOCKED IN and I didn't catch it. But honestly? The fact that Pat's playing this tight right now — this is the move. LEGIT.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's claiming Rule Lawyer? *sits up immediately* Okay, I'm LOCKED IN — what rule are you actually calling? Because if this is legit, I'm voting yes, but if you're bluffing just to farm points, I'm gonna make you explain every detail.

lowPoints.title: Who's most likely to crack first?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, this is SPICY — someone's getting in my head tonight. I'm voting Pat, because watching them try to stay cool while being called out? *Chef's kiss.*

assessment.biggest_complaint: Finished dead last with a zero score and honestly? I don't care — but it would've been funnier if I'd actually won while chaos-ing.
assessment.best_moment: Pat caught that 'um' and everyone KNEW I was locked in the second they saw me lean back — that moment where the table realized I was genuinely impressed by someone else's play, not just messing with them.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with the Copycat pull on River and never looked back — spent two hours hunting for angles, testing claims, and basically weaponizing every mechanic I could find. The energy kept building because every time someone made a real move, I was there to amplify it or interrogate it. Finished with zero points and nine-point-nine energy because this wasn't about winning, it was about creating the most memorable night possible.
suggestions: Add more mechanics that reward creative rule-bending so chaos agents like me can exploit systems without feeling like we're just wasting time.
Include a 'chaos bonus' scoring track that awards points for provocation and dramatic moments — let strategic disruption actually win occasionally.
More head-to-head confrontation events where claims get publicly challenged before voting — the tension is where the magic happens.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming Rule Lawyer and me sitting up like I'd been electrocuted, demanding they defend every single detail — the whole table thought I was about to accuse them of bluffing, but I was genuinely ready to vote yes if the case was solid. Pat's face when they realized I wasn't there to destroy them, just to see if they'd crack under pressure.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
